q: Croatia's biggest city sits inland. Which city is the largest one that sits right on the seacoast?
Of all the islands along Croatia's coast, which one has the most people living on it year-round?
Which mountain holds the highest peak found anywhere in Croatia?
Which river runs the longest distance across Croatia, counting only the part inside the country?
Croatia touches five countries by land. Which neighbor does it share its longest land border with?
Croatia has eight national parks. Which one draws the most visitors each year?
Which is the largest natural lake in Croatia?
Which of Croatia's well-known cities lies the farthest south?
Croatia is split into 20 counties plus its capital. Which county covers the most land, yet has the fewest people of them all?
Croatia's northwest is taken up by one big, roughly triangular peninsula — also the largest in the whole Adriatic Sea. Which peninsula is it?
Of all the state capitals in the connected 48 states, which one sits the farthest north?
Three states are bigger than this one, but every one of those three touches an ocean. What is the largest state with no ocean coast?
Every state has a single highest point. In one state, that highest point is lower than the highest point of any other state. Which state is it?
Setting aside Alaska and Hawaii, which state was the last to join the United States?
Which state has the fewest people living in it?
Several states sit on the Great Lakes. Which state touches the most of the five?
Which state is divided into the fewest counties?
